name: Lark / Feishu
description: Thin Lark/Feishu AgentApp for the official lark-cli. It teaches agents to discover and use the CLI's own commands, schemas, embedded skills, auth flow, and risk signals instead of reimplementing Lark APIs.

references.title: Lark/Feishu operation entrypoint
references.description: Portable guidance for using lark-cli as the authoritative discovery, auth, schema, and execution surface.

references.title: Official lark-cli source and discovery contract
references.description: What the AgentApp relies on from the official Lark/Feishu CLI and what it intentionally does not duplicate.

references.title: CLI-assisted user consent flow
references.description: Portable auth guidance based on lark-cli config/auth commands and device-flow continuation.

references.title: Live validation discipline
references.description: Real-device checks for installed CLI discovery, auth status, read actions, write previews, and risk handling.

cli.name: lark-cli
cli.installHint: If lark-cli is missing, inspect the official install guidance with `npx @larksuite/cli@latest install` and then verify with `lark-cli --version`, `lark-cli doctor`, and `lark-cli --help`. Do not silently install or update unless the current user task calls for making the capability usable.
cli.discoveryHint: Run `lark-cli --help`, `lark-cli doctor`, `lark-cli whoami`, `lark-cli skills list`, `lark-cli skills read <skill>`, service-specific `lark-cli <domain> --help`, and `lark-cli schema <service.resource.method>` as needed. Prefer shortcuts before typed API commands, and typed API commands before raw `lark-cli api`.

auth.callbackHint: Use the CLI-reported authorization URL and continuation instructions as opaque output. For agent mode, prefer `lark-cli auth login --no-wait --json`, let the user complete consent in a browser where their Lark/Feishu account is available, then resume with `lark-cli auth login --device-code <DEVICE_CODE>`. If a CLI path uses localhost callback instead, complete consent only where that callback is reachable.
